text: The location on good homes Road Amel and cat are awesome sand lake rd location Jayson as well great customers sevice hats off to you thanks for always going the extra mile and over exceeding the expectations awesome customer service thank you John Woodward.

Scammers STAY AWAY! In the course of 6 months I’ve paid tmobile over $2000 for service. Unexplainable fees/taxes/charges. I’ve contacted tmobile since I received my first bill. Left the store paying $500 in less than a month I received a bill for over $300 customer service “wheels and deals” you but expect your bull to stay outrageous for poor cellular service. I absolutely HATE tmobile and have contacted the BBB to file a complaint for their scamming business practices. Another thing, I was promised by the store employee my bill would be $145/month everything included, my bill has been $200 and above since I joined tmobile. Horrible horrible experience if I could go back to that day I would have NEVER stepped foot in tmobile store

The customer service was amazing they were understanding and helped me figure out the issue. I didn’t get to thank them or solve the problem. But thank you!! - Kelela.

Zero if I could❗️Terrible customer service and terrible connectivity issues❗️Data will shut down for no reason at all❗️And the thing that I hate most, is having to turn off and turn my phone back on 5-10 times daily because it freezes up❗️Dropped and too many spotty areas, where you get no coverage❗️Been with all the major phone carrriers, but T-Mobile is by far at the bottom❗️Company needs to collapse❗️

This company is a disaster!!

Everything is hours on the phone with them. Hidden charges, removing 365 protection plan without my consent. The website is pathetic can not get into the section to change password. 
I got a nice lady who spoke clearly which is extremely rare most are from India making it super difficult I know one of the owners of T-MOBILE personally he is Indian.     
I almost reached out maybe I will and rant to him what bad experience with T-MOBILE .

They hate me. I’m pretty sure it’s a gay thing.  I have been a customer for nearly 20 years. Never a late payment, purchaser of new phones and accessories nearly every year. All I want is to upgrade my iPhone 12 Mini to an iPhone 13 Mini in red, and they won’t do it unless I pay them 420 dollars. Why can’t I just send them back my current iPhone 12? I begged, I pleaded. I just wanted to trade up. They should let me for my loyalty at least. They say NO! The man took pleasure in refusing to provide satisfactory service to the type of customer ANY company would want! But Verizon says they would be glad to have me.  I told Sprint but they will probably be glad to see me go. They hate me. Because I am gay. It’s too bad.

I tried their service and it didn't work so they were going to refund my 175 activation fee. which they did NOT. Instead they canceled the account and proceeded to charge my card another 160 for no charges because the account was closed weeks earlier. 2 months and no refunds. crappy company

THEY HAVE THE WORST CUSTOMER SERVICE EVER, SINCE MARCH I HAVE BEEN TRYING TO SOLVE A PROBLEM WITH THEM ABOUT SOME PHONE LINES THAT I NEVER ACTIVATED AND NO ONE HAS BEEN ABLE TO SOLVE THE PROBLEM EVEN THE SUPERVISORS. I HAVE SPOKE WITH MORE THAN 10 DIFFERENT PEOPLE ABOUT IT. 
THEY HAVE HARMED ME. THEY HAVE ROBBED ME. I HAVE HAD TO PAY FOR TAXES AND DOWN PAYMENT FOR PHONES WHICH I HAVE NEVER RECEIVED BECAUSE THEY HAD AN INTERNAL ISSUE AND NEVER HAVE SENT THE DEVICES TO ME. THEY EVEN HAVE SENT ME TO A DEBT COLLECTOR FOR SOMETHING THAT I’M NOT SUPPOSED TO PAY. I DON’T OWN THEM BUT I HAVE BEEN OBLIGED TO MAKE A PAYMENT TO AVOID MORE AND MORE FEES. 
HOW CAN THEY CHARGE ME FOR SOMETHING THAT I NEVER GOT IT? 
THEY ARE THE WORST COMPANY I HAVE EVER WORKED WITH.
I HAVE BEEN TELLING ALL MY FAMILY AND FRIENDS TO CANCEL THEIR CELL PLANS WITH THEM AND NEVER GET INVOLVED WITH THIS UNFAIR COMPANY.

Do not go to T-mobile. For one they are liers its not unlimited data. You get 100mb out of which you can only use 35mb it's a joke! T-mobile is for poor Black people living off the government! If you truly want the best switch to ATT it's the same price and they offer the best service hands down! T-mobile should be shut down!

The promotion is a joke. I paid for everything including taxes, new service, new cover....everything but the $400 offered for my trade in. Yet they now state I owe them $3.14 for then next 24 months. That $75 is a "promotion" and what I now owe. The twist of words and also I see they forged my signature on their agreement papers. I wanted just to have a monthly service charge. NOT LOCKED INTO AN AGREEMENT.

I was a sprint customer that was moved to T-Mobile. T-Mobile's cell network drops constantly. I do not have the coverage I had with Sprint. I never had a problem with the Sprint cell network. The T-Mobile cell network is the worst coverage I have ever had. This has been going on since I moved to T-Mobile over a month ago. The coverage T-Mobile claims to have is a lie.  I will be changing service providers. Do not waste your time with this provider.

T MOBILE – THE BEST (IF YOU’RE A CRIMINAL!) otherwise THE WORST

Want a free cell phone? Order one on-line from T-Mobile, using someone else’s name and the numbers of their checking account. Ship it to your own address, no need to worry, because T-Mobile will NEVER CHECK. The poor schmuck whose name you stole will be on the hook, my evidence to the contrary be damned.  IMHO, and that of my attorney, T-Mobile is aiding and abetting a criminal. 

Why do I feel this way? An identity thief I’ll call “AC” targeted me, my husband and my children over the past 2 years, documented by a 40-page police report. Her crimes include, but aren’t limited to, wire fraud, bank fraud, identity theft, mail fraud, and impersonating a public official. Recently, she purchased a T-Mobile phone on-line using my name and bank account and had the merchandise shipped to her home (as listed in the police report – over 30 miles from my home of 35 years). T-Mobile’s “investigation” determined that I am liable for the purchase (and T-Mobile gets to keep my money) even though I do not now have, never have had, and never will have a T-Mobile account. Multiple attempts to clear this up has led them to double down. They won’t even give me the phone number of the headset they claim “I” purchased. 

Call me crazy, but if tasked to “investigate” who made a suspect purchase, I’d determine who resides at the home where the goods were shipped. In their ongoing effort to aid and abet a criminal, T-Mobile disregarded this “clue” entirely. (Going all in on illogic, they conceded two subsequent monthly service charges on the same account were fraudulent because “AC” used her own name for those charges.)  I’ve wasted hours with T-Mobile and my bank, demonstrating – via their own documents - that “my” purchase was delivered to the person about to be criminally charged (I can only pray) with stealing my identity – all to no avail. 

T-Mobile has no right to force me to buy a phone for “AC” – who deceived, betrayed, exploited and attacked my family for years – and, as of now, continues to do so. Why stop, when (so far) she’s suffered no consequences? I have no doubt that she’ll unleash the same hell she’s put us through on the next unfortunate, trusting and naive person who crosses her path. I wouldn’t wish it on my worst enemy.

Please share my experience with anybody evaluating – or having issues with - cell service providers or identity theft.  As stated above, I’ve retained an attorney. If you’ve been victimized by T-Mobile, leave a message here. There’s strength in numbers.

Been on the phone with t-mobile/ sprint about my services for 2 days now and I'm still having the same issues everyone passing me to next person and I had one rep go as far of I knew how to read when explaining my problems I'm paying for a services that I can't use been a customer since 2004 being treated like an imbecile

Tmobile is absolute garbage .  i am now getting lawyer to litigate against them

edits.text:
Absolutely the most incompetent customer service and tech support I have ever experienced bar none.   I would not trust my personal information with these bozos.  They leaked private texts to all of my friend's contacts, and they try to charge me for internet that they never sent me a modem for.  I have spent several hours trying to sort things out with internet, giving the same sensitive personal information to several staff members per sitting, just to have them open multiple accounts under my name, send me bills for services I have never received, and deny me the service because of faulty technical issues or just total incompetence. 
 Clown school rejects.  Don't bother with it, unless you're a glutton for punishment.
I'm still being charged even after cancelling my service.  The internet service was worse than the public wifi near me, and I had an ethernet cable plugged directly into my computer.   Scumbags!

text: Worst customer service I've had in years.  I was with AT&T, good signal and good customer service, but they were pricey.  I switched to Verizon in 2019, they had good customer service but terrible signal in my area (Norfolk, VA).  That made me switch to T-Mobile last month (Aug-2022).  I immediately had customer service issues.  They recorded the wrong phones I was trading in, didn't send the trade in shipping box, messed up pairing my apple watch, and added a line I didn't ask for.  I called back and tried to fix it, and they made a bunch of other mistakes.  I went to the store, but they said because I ordered the phones and setup service over the phone, I had to call customer support.  Two days later, I received a text from T-Mobile that said I hadn't turned my trade-in phones yet and I had to bring them into a store.  I went to the store, and they wouldn't accept them.  They did print me a shipping label but couldn't give me a box to ship them in.  I had to figure that out on my own.  I've traded iPhones into Apple, AT&T, and Verizon -- all of them sent a trade-in kit in the mail, but not T-Mobile.  It's been one month since I started service, I have called customer service half a dozen times and I've been into stores twice.  The errors on my account still are not fixed, and I'm waiting for a follow up call that will probably never come.  I'd stay away from T-Mobile.  At this point, I'd rather be back with Verizon even with their poor signal.

If I could give zero stars I would. I tried the service out for not even an hour took the SIM card out and was able to get an even lower monthly with my original service. I sent back the hot spot device with in a month. I had zero minutes , phone and texted used and 0 data usage tell me why I have a bill for 257 dollars? They refuse to make it zero! BEWARE OF T MOBILE. When I call to file a dispute they hang up on me and act like they can’t hear me.

I had an email account tied to my sons account at one time where he was the primary.  He cancelled my phone line and I got my own account and used my same email.  For over 2 years I have been unable to get to my own account because it automatically is tied to his phone, not even my old one.  I have tried to get this resolved and they keep saying only he can change this, for his own protection.  I was not told this would be an issue when I signed up for my own account.  I found out today, because of this he can get into my account, which I NEVER authorized he could do because of this email issue.  I can no longer get to his account because verifying codes get sent to his phone,  To get to my account they suggest maybe setting up a new email account.  That doesn't resolve an unauthorized person getting to my account.  They are protecting his rights and not mine to have sole access to my email account.  Legal team needs to be made aware.  I was not told until today he has access to my primary account and I do not.

I have had tmoble for over 5 years, used to be decent phone option, decent service at a decent price, every since 5g came around all my phone does is buffer, I have the option on my note 20 ultra to run 2g 3g 3g lte or 5g, the only problem is that they all don't work. And when calling tmoble for help....well turn off ur phone then turn it back on, thats all they got for help....As soon as I get my phones paid off I plan unlocking the phones on my plan and going to a different carrier, hope this helps people from having a headache like I have had since 5g came out. Stay blessed and have. A great day...

Switched to T Mobile mid June, since paying my last Bill, they keep telling me I'm past due. I spend the morning texting them, since their website won't allow my log in all Labor Day weekend, and the idiot on the other end tried telling me my 1st billing cycle was in May, when I didn't even gt their service until mid June.

He tried offering me $20, so I'd only be getting screwed out of $50 instead of the entire $70 they're claiming.

I guess plans for Seniors means we're morons.

I'm tired of arguing with them. I'll be switching carriers (and told them so, they didn't care), and have gathered my evidence for court, which is where this will end-up as I'm sure they'll claim I've violated my contract, when it is actually THEM who has dropped the ball.

FYI, their site still doesn't work (even after telling them) and I have screenshots to prove it.

DO NOT USE T MOBILE!

T-mobile is by far the best decision I’ve ever made for home internet. Very friendly customer service staff, I’ve been with them almost a year and even when short on cash they are always understanding. Good service to switch to for easy installation as well as excellent WiFi network.
Service is terrible,  service dept is horrible and the stores are worthless
I called 611 got a operator that did not understand anything I had to go to the store to fix my

T-Mobile is a trash company run by trash people with sub-trash service. A generous (to T-Mobile) estimate would be that about 70% of the time, I can access their data. Conversely, around 30% of the time, I get no data or service at all. This applies to SMS messaging, social media apps, internet browsers, Spotify, and anything else I may try to access on their service. I genuinely feel scammed by them and I cannot believe that their are allowed to remain in business, given that their product is essentially useless. I know that their Magenta Max plan is alluring, being $85/mo, unlimited data and free Netflix, but, if you have the funds and are able to get a cellular line on literally any other service, I implore you to do so! Do not give T-Mobile your money! You will regret it!

Run fast from Tmobile! They have you spend hours troubleshooting with non-technical people before they send you to a technical person who still can not fix your problem. 

I can not access my voice mail since upgrading to the flip 4. Spent 1 hour on the phone with  a customer service rep who tried to troubleshoot my problem.  It was way over her head. I finally asked her to escalate the call.  Then I spent another 1 1/2 hours with a tech support and they couldn't get it to work.

They said my Sim card doesn't allow voicemail and they don't know how to fix it. I pay for voice mail but I have no access to it. 

I am not a happy customer now that they are T-mobile. I was with Sprint for over 10 years. I've always had a good experience with Sprint.  

Me and my 8 devices is going to a new carrier because I've had problems ever since they merged with Tmobile.  Tmobile sucks.
New charge added 9/3/22. $35 per line for customer service calls. Yes I said PER LINE

Transaction occurred on 7/9/22.

My family and I decided to change cell providers switching to T-Mobile.  We went to the store located 4305 State Bridge Rd #104A, Alpharetta, GA 30022, to complete the transaction.  The young lady working that day provided us all the information needed to move forward.  The offer included "credits" towards our bills for the phones we where trading in which totaled $3,100. The credits would be spread out monthly for the next 24 months.  They gave us $800 for 3 of the phone, $400 for another, and $300 for the last.   All was good we paid the money on the spot owed wiped and handed over 5 phones and left after a few hours of setup.  The bill for the first month of service came in and no "credits" where applied. We called, they explained it would take another billing cycle to reflect, not worries we moved on.  The second bill came in and credit where applied but only for the prorated amount if $2,800 not the original $3,100 outlined in the contract.  We called again and they said that the store was not permitted to offer the $300 for the 5th IPhone and nothing could be done on their end.  They said the store manager could provide a credit to fix the error.   The manager was notified and the customer service representative said "the manager will take care of you".  They requested I stop in and talk to the manager named "A" in the next few days.  I stopped in yesterday and talked to A and he advised that he was able to give me a $50 credit on my bill.  I explained to A that the credit missing was for $300 and we needed to fix the issue in full.  A shrugged his shoulder and told me "it is what it is".  He said he would see if he could do anything else but would not commit to a date.  I am filing this complaint as I want the $250 still owed to me.  By taking possession of my phone, not willing to return it, and not paying me what was outlined in the contract, it amounts to theft.  I will take this matter to small claims court, based on the documentation I have, unless resolved in a timely manner. 

I would avoid this location if you expect an honest business transaction to occur.

was a sprint customer and had no issues for years . The trouble started this summer with low to no signal at home. Online customer service is a joke they try and say its the phone then after you do all the things they want you to do to it they admit its a service issue. still nothing is done about it.

T-Mobile is by far the worst customer service experience I have ever had from any company, full stop.

After switching to their internet, which was easy and good, the nightmare of switching over our phone service was a disaster. The original sales rep who sold us our phone lied to us about the trade-in process. This has resulted in 10+ hours on the phone with reps and multiple in-store visits. To correct it, they had to send an entirely brand-new phone, and they still couldn't handle that correctly. There is too much to even write: inability to correctly port old numbers, overcharging on multiple billing issues, pushy sales reps who act like their essentials plan doesn't exist in an attempt to get you to do Magenta Max, inability to communicate internally in their company, inability to issue a correct pin number, inability to handle a return without losing it in their warehouse, inability to understand American English which makes phone calls twice as long...I spent two and a half hours on one phone call a few weeks ago, and I even heard a rooster in the background when I called, I kid you not!

Most of the reps are offshore and poorly trained. The in-store reps are annoyed by their company's poorly trained phone reps. It's a disaster. Yes, you will save a few dollars over Spectrum, Verizon, etc., but at what cost? You will waste hours and your sanity dealing with a company that has grown too fast to support its customer base. I cannot wait until my 24 billing cycles are done so I can never come back. What an absolute disaster. T-Mobile is the K-Mart of cell phone companies.

Ever since the merger, T-mobile customer Service has went down the drain!!!!!!! These agents are all RIDE, spoiled brats on their first job, with ZERO adult supervision!!! T-mobile is in a bad spot...looking for a new carrier now...so much for 15+ years of loyalty!!!! RUDE= LESS CUSTOMERS!!!
Terrible customer service. No service ad sub standard Cel  strength

We have had Sprint for several years and never had an issue with neither service nor receiving our trade-in credits for phones that were upgraded. Since T-Mobile has taken over three out of three of our trade-in phones were canceled for the trade-in credit stating falsely that the phones were not in acceptable condition. The phones were in great condition. They couldn’t even keep their lies straight. The first two phones we were told had water damage, then when the third one came up as an acceptable condition and I brought up the first two I was told that the first two were because of a wrong model number and cracked screen. They are nothing but crooks!!! Boy how I miss Sprint. I would also give them zero stars if it was possible.

I ordered a  Mi-Fi  for internet $50 a month but then was told I was buying the unit so a $7 charge would be on my bill to pay for it until it was paid off. after a couple months the unit started acting up and I was constantly having to restart it and after calling 3 times to resolve the problem they said it was defective and they would send me a replacement, that I would have to pick up in the store. When I got to the store I had to wait 15 minutes for the safe to open to get my unit. while waiting they suggested I try the gateway home internet, I was told previously that the gateway wouldn't work at the location of my home. I took it home and it worked. I wanted to return the defective Mifi and just use the gateway. I was told I couldn't return it, I could exchange it for the replacement unit and I  still had to pay for it (over $200)  I figured since I had to pay for it anyway I should exchange it for the replacement since it was defective. After 20 minutes of processing the exchange and I was ready to leave with the new unit, They informed me that I had to pay $20 for the new warranty on the new unit. I said I don't want the warranty and they said I had to pay it or I can't get the unit. I refused and left. There was no guarantee that the new unit wouldn't be defective. I believe I paid $20 for the warranty on the first unit which is supposed to replace it for free, but they in essence are charging me $20 for the replacement. $20 for something I no longer needed because I now have the gateway. So now I get to pay over $200 for a defective unit. They are Thieves. This is Horrible customer service.

worst ever , been with them for 10 yrs switched recently and they couldn't process a partial refund in over 2.5 months had to call several times; every time a rep screws up and the next one explains the past screw up ..really !! ...  never coming back to this BS company

Off shore customer service that input customers complaint into their computer and reads back what the computer says Home internet is a sham. Would have had to use it like a hot spot bring it from room to room. 3 days of talking  to Customer support with no solution.

the worst operator ever… tarifs are too expensive without any reason, their costumer service is tragical: you must always pay attention cause they dont tell you if you didnt pay them money (even if there is no reason why you should pay to them; from me they wanted money even if i canceled all of my services from them…) 
worst operator in the world, i ll never come back to them, terrible expierence

. T mobile just wants the money and can care less about customer service. Maybe Sprint will do better.
T-mobile has got to be the worst company on earth! Do not use them! 19 years with sprint and then they merged with t-mobile. They are awful, awful, awful.

LONG WAIT... please be prepared to be on hold for hours before a live person answers even when you request a call back.  The chat wait time is just as long.  I was calling about an over charge on my bill of about $200.00. And I am still holding for them to make the correction.... dying slowly waiting... hair is now graying... I fell asleep woke up and was still on hold.

We have been with T-Mobile for several month now   At first it was great told many of our friends about how great the service was.  We even stopped using our cable service us T-Mobile WiFi only great tv service also.   BUT several times now we have had NO WIFI SERVICE. In the middle of a project on tablet WiFi shuts down in the middle of a movie WiFi shuts down when we call to complain we get the same answer we are working on new tower   That is is just a line they give you just to satisfy their costumers. But just so you at T-Mobile know we are not stupid we think you just do not what to fix the problem. We should of NEVER NEVER left our previous WiFi service.  We have our phones and our WiFi with you our phone have also been acting up.  FIX THE PROBLEM WITH YOU SERVICE AND YOU ALSO NEED BETTER CUSTOMER SERVICE SO STOP GIVING UP THE SAME ANSWER EVER TIME WE ARE NOT STUPID.    Now are you going to do anything about this problem or not

I really wanted this to work for us but it just isn't meant to be. I signed up with T-Mobile and got a Google pixel 6 for myself and my husband. Here we are a few months later and we HATE these phones. T-Mobile tells me I cannot exchange these phone for an entirely different brand phone unless I pay $400+ for each phone. The problem: Google Pixel 6 has a hardware issue with the screen proximity sensor. Your screen is supposed to go dark (inactive) while you are talking on it thus preventing you from inadvertently touching things on your screen. This sensor does not work correctly on this phone and screen becomes active while you are talking on it. Also, while you are talking on it if a message or notification comes through it activates the screen that way also. So with this issue when you are talking on the phone you will randomly put the call on hold,mute, or video call the person you are speaking with. By the time you realize this has happened the person has already hung up because they could no longer hear you and assume the call was disconnected. It's not always so simple to just call someone back. Example: you were speaking with a business that took you 10 minutes of waiting to get to the right person, doctor's office, your work, etc... My husband uses his phone for his work and this has become a huge problem. T-Mobile told me I had 20 days to return the phone. Well it takes a while to realize what is happening because at first for awhile you assume it was a dropped call on your or the other person's behalf. Then when you realize what's happening you assume an update will eventually be out because you see online that others are having this problem. Finally you do some major time consuming research and find out that this is a hardware issue that an update cannot fix and that the phone is becoming notorious for. Now it's too late to do anything. Oh, but they will exchange the phone for another of the same model. And same hardware, same problem. Funny thing too is that the problem happened WHILE I was on the phone with T-Mobile and they kept saying hello, hello until I looked at my phone and unmuted it and said well there is a perfect example! They are apologetic and nice, but they will not do ANYTHING about it. The only real fix is that they let you exchange your phone for a different one. And I'm willing to pay the difference for a good phone like a Samsung 22 or a iphone 13. But nope, they say no can do until either my two years is up or I pay the phones off. We were with our last carrier for 15 years so T-Mobile could have gained a long term customer here but I will NEVER stay with them nor recommend them to anyone. They cannot tell me they were unaware of these issues because so many people have had this problem yet they continue to sell this phone with a smile on their faces to their customers. Thanks for nothing T-Mobile.
Contact said if you return equipment within 10 days no charge. Returned equipment next day. Called 3 times and complained about a bill we received. Now in collections.

I have had Sprint for 10 plus years. I rarely had issues and never had issues with my service. Ever since Sprint merged with T-Mobile, my service is AWFUL. I lose internet every 5 minutes, calls drop out of nowhere. I will be switching to a new carrier. I am so disappointed because I absolutely loved Sprint.

Since T-Mobile merged with Sprint, the cell service in my home goes from poor to nonexistent. Yesterday, I spent three hours on the phone (from my back yard, due to poor reception), only to end up with a phone with no cell service at all and no ability to message. I went to the T-Mobile store, only to find out my phone had been reported lost or stolen. This had to have been done by the folks on the other end of the phone earlier. After a considerable amount of time, they were able to remove the block, but told me it would be 24 hours before my phone was working again. This was not acceptable to me, because T Mobile was the one who caused it. I told them I refused to leave the store without a working phone. If they couldn’t fix it immediately, I suggested a loaner phone or a burner phone or something (which they could not do, even though they had a whole store full of phones). After three hours inside the store, it was finally resolved. I spent a total of six hours on this issue, only to end up with the same poor service.

I recently ordered and switched my carrier to tmobile but its been over 2weeks so i cancelled and wanted to speak with a supervisor and was told they didnt want to talk to me. The supervisor is Vianey. On top of not recieving or talking to a supervisor i wont recieve my money for 5 days. Tmobile does not help or make a customer feel like tgey even care. I wouldnt even bother going thru tmobile cuz they treat you like crap and make you feel like your not important!!

I should have never switched! I was supposed to have a bill of $108 for 3 lines total each month. I'm getting bills for $250-$300 which has put me behind and now getting ready to be disconnected! I have called and called and what they tell me is some bs about "1 time fees". Really? I have never been in a bad way with any phone company. I've had same number for 10 years and now I'm about to lose it because of all their hidden fees! I cannot get ahead with this company. I don't recommend them at all!

Avoid this companies 5G home internet like your life depends on it.  Worked decent the first month & then after that its been absolutely horrific.  The router would constantly go down & I spent a total of 8 hours on the phone with tech support, which absolutely is useless by the way,  they would just have me unplug it and plug it back in until it worked. The final step today was to reset it to factory settings after nearly 3 hours in the phone, worked 5 mins, then out it went.  Finally went to a location and they exchanged it but I've been demanding a credit of one month.  Long story, they don't give a crap about their customers despite saying they do.  I can't recommend any service that this money stealing company has.  If you enjoying paying for poor service, terrible customer service and wasting your valuable time, then by all means this is the company for you!

In our area the quote that they cover all of the US does not make sense. At best we get 2 bars in Lewiston NY, but at worst they tell you to dial over your own internet.
I am waiting to get out of this grey cellular area, where you actually get more signal from Canada than the US
when someone installs a new tower or set of antennas for our area.

If i could give no stars I absolutely would.  Awful customer service; often dishonest, and the best part is that because I switched carriers and ended up cancelling my account this situation gets even funnier.   I informed T-Mobile about a month ago that I would be leaving them for Verizon as a result of service not working properly; phone freezing, poor customer service, and the fact I had spoken to 15 different people over the span of about a week only to get about 16 different stories.   They instead of sending me my final bill immediately sent it to a debt collector that they are now trying to pretend like it didn't actually happen even though I got a random notice in the mail without ever having received a final bill or anything (online access was disabled when I cancelled the account.)  I would not recommend at all they are very sly and dishonest. If you haven't sent someone a final paper bill how do you expect someone to pay the bill?   Also despite being told numerous stories my account was never actually credited for the many weeks my service would go from 3 bars to barely half a bar sounding like the Verizon guy that shouts in that commercial 'Can you hear me now trying to connect to their own customer service (if you'll call it service.)

-5 stars to T mobil bunch of rats 20 years with t mobil canceled mi service. and still reciving Bill's  for $325.00 dollars .I move to mint and I saving lot of money. t mobil is %$%%<<&^& .

Horrible experience! My phone has not worked since I purchased it due to a tower being down. I spoke to over 30 techs and supervisors with no results. After 25 years with Verizon i switched my family's 5 phones over to T-Mobile. T-Mobile Boise corporate staff is super friendly & helpful to a degree but at the 1-800 corporate level the switching over of the phones is convoluted and extremely problematic and difficult to resolve basic issues.  The phones are packaged without basic adapters and USB connections.  This presents other headaches for the customer to deal with. We're on our 5th day of issues.

 Folks; think twice before you switch carriers and ask questions as to how to charge phones, what is  the out the door cost, what's the return policy... We've saved all our packaging and if we can't get our phones setup properly we will boldly return everything for 100% money back within 30 days.  Stay tuned....

Overcharged and no refund!!! Dishonest behaviours

I used T-Mobile service for 2 years and decided to close my account early 2022 as I was not satisfied with the customer service. When I closed the account, I paid the dues. Soon after that, I received a bill of $50, I called and paid. In a month, I received another bill of $20, I called and paid again. In another month, I received another bill of $5. I got upset, so I send a check of $5 and wrote a letter telling them that I would not keep paying them for the service that I cancelled a couple of months back. Finally, T-Mobile stopped sending me bills. 

Surprisingly, in another month, they sent me a mail saying that I have a balance of negative $75. So, I called and asked for the refund, I was told they didn’t find my account. I wrote a letter and told what happened. I got a reply from an offline service expert telling me that they sent me the refund in a citiBank prepaid card in March. As I never received it, so I replied. Then I received another letter from him repeating the same information. I replied again and said if they didn’t want to refund me, just let me know so I could stop expecting. Again, I received his reply, this time, asking me to call Northlane about the citiBank prepaid card. I called and talked to 2 different agents, they didn’t find my information in their system which means T-Mobile lied to me as they didn’t ask Northlane to send me the refund in a citiBank prepaid card. I called T-Mobile and asked them to send a request to Northlane, they didn’t find my account and they can’t send the request. 
T-Mobile lied all the time. T-Mobile closed my account and delete the key information to make the refund request. Then T-Mobile lied and told me that they sent me a citiBank prepaid card. Finally, T-Mobile asked to check with Northlane about the prepaid card but Northlane didn’t have any of my information. T-Mobile is such a liar. Why don’t be honest?? If you don’t want to refund me, just let me know and that’s it. It’s so simple. This issue harassed me for half a year as T-Mobile kept sending me bills and mails.  

If you have any doubts or questions, let me know.

T-Mobile has terrible service! I had a prepaid phone with T-Mobile. I go to their site today trying to log in with the phone number but was told the account did not exist. I called the service and verified that they had given the number to someone else in another service. I had this phone number with T-Mobile Prepaid account and had money to keep it last for a while. I have several other online accounts associated with this phone number. Even the money was used up, they should notify me to fill more. No, they just give the number away to another service without my knowing it. And they told me to deal with the other service to get the number back! I have no business with the other service! Now I am just totally helpless!

I have had the pre-paid plan for quite a few years with Walmart family mobile / powered by T-Mobile. I have the unlimited talk, text & data plan.  When I call about my iPhone stopped sending texts to android phones again for the 3rd time over the past 3 months or so, they either hung up on me when I am expressing the frustration of the matter, & they never call back the number from all the information I keep giving both levels. Another problem they keep connecting me to the computer & not a human most of the times, even after asking them to stay on the phone until I am connected with live person. It’s been one lie after another, & the problem ever got solved. This provider sucks In my experience, & apparently many of the reviews I read, they were some bad experiences too.  One thing I learn from this is to read the reviews before paying for services.  I will research other plans elsewhere & switch over by the end of this cycle I already paid for, though I am not getting the full service I paid for.  I really should be compensated for all 3 times I wasted many, many hrs. Trying to get this company to fix their data problem for all 3 occasions.  When I mentioned that it of my frustration, the agent hung up the phone, & after then the call were not going through to the upper data level.  These people seems to suck & is showing a lack of experience in  performing the duties they are paid for.  Perhaps the company need to retrain their staffs before they loose   Many customers.

To whom it may concern,

The Samsung/ T-Mobile watch really appealed to me. Last year I had serious open-heart surgery, and the result could easily have gone either way. When I was offered this watch, the salesperson explained to me that it could monitor my heartbeat, measure my blood pressure, and various other biometrics, including sleep. Most important, I would be able to dial ''911'' and the ''GPS'' would direct first responders to my location. After a life and death experience, a ''safety net'' is most appreciated...

As a rehabilitation tool, the watch can monitor the total number of steps I perform daily to assist in my recovery. As sold to me, this watch was paramount to my well-being and future progression as a cardiac patient. After reading this, I am sure that every ''at risk'' person would rush to purchase one. This is where the nightmare starts...

I bought the watch and was excited to begin my recovery. Initially, the watch would charge for a short time but would not retain the charge. The employee at the T-Mobile store told us I charged the watch for too long. ''The watch was not meant to be continuously on the charger.'' It turned out not to be the case. The watch was defective. Although only a month old, T-Mobile charged me $20 to replace it!

The second watch was no better. For some mysterious reason, the watch cannot monitor my steps. The most infuriating aspect of this is the watch would measure my steps on a ''hit or miss'' basis. Mostly miss and only for brief periods of time. Hours were spent talking to customer service to no avail. Reboot. Reboot. Reboot. This was the response. The watch would only work sporadically. 

Finally, I sent the watch to the Samsung repair center to be evaluated. Dealing with the repair center was a debacle in itself. The center e-mailed us the incorrect barcode for the return so we traveled 25 miles only to discover the incorrect code. At $5.50 a gallon for gas, we probably purchased the watch a second time! The repair center contacted us and informed us that the watch is now fine. I am excited to regain my ''safety net''. Lo and behold, the watch works worse than ever! That means never ...

One more try with customer service and the familiar Reboot. Reboot. Reboot. As a former customer service executive for a Fortune 500 Company, I am appalled at the customer service by both Samsung and T-Mobile. This watch was presented as a lifeline for my peace of mind and healthy recovery from a major surgery. I look at my watch that cost me hours of my time wasted with customer service, untold miles of driving to the nearest T-Mobile, and the $10 per month I am still being charged for service that was never delivered. As a loyal customer of Samsung for many years, I am disgusted and ashamed how such an established brand can so thoroughly tarnish their image. 



To facilitate the generation of seamless 
customer service and a product that   
lives up to the brand Samsung.

Sincerely,

Irv

T-MOBILE HAS BEEN RIPPING ME OFF FOR OVER 3 YEARS!
I purchased a new phone 3 years ago and was offered a samsung watch. T-Mo/sprint paired my watch to my phone and I was on my way. they had set up a plan so the watch would work independently without the phone. I was never notified of this and they have been charging me $10 a month for the plan. I never once used data because they did not fulfill their part of the contract and failed to actually set up the phone line for the watch, but have been charging me for over 3 years at $10 a month.
Fast forward to 2 weeks ago. My wife and I went in to get her a new phone. same deal, they talked her into a new watch as well with an independent line for it. I thought it would be cool to set up my watch so I can just leave it at my desk and walk away from my phone from time to time. I was then notified that i did have a plan for the watch but that it was never activated by them. the rep assured my wife that we would get the money back spent on the plan which was over $360.00 but now the upper management is failing to take responsibility for their failure to follow through on a contract . T-Mo failed to set up the watch plan and now they tell me that my phone cant be set up on the existing line and that I have 2 choices. They can cancel the line and set it up as new and give me 100.00 dollar credit or sell me a new watch at 50% and get that one set up instead. I've been paying for a plan that never worked due to their lack of follow through and assuring that my plan was set up correctly.

T-MOBILE HAS BEEN RIPPING ME OFF FOR OVER 3 YEARS!
they are the worst , the rep made a deal with me , and the next day said they will not do the deal , after 9 fn years of not one late bill, 2 hrs later and 2 supervisors later ...  crickets...

On Saturday, 20 August at 1:30 p.m., my wife and I went to T-Mobile store located on Laclede Station Road in Webster Groves, MO.  We had purchased Smart Phones from T-Mobile in 2019, and had a few questions about operating and updating.  A gentleman there named "Ron" spent at least a half hour answering our questions and suggested ways for us to get better use out of our phones.  He was most gracious and nice, and extremely smart, and we left T-Mobile well-informed, more comfortable with our phones, and ready to upgrade with T-Mobile next year.  He was not pushy at all, and we definitely will be dedicated T-Mobile customers from now on out.  Thank You, Ron!  The McCormicks of Saint Louis, MO.

Ever since Sprint has merged with T-mobile I’ve had nothing but problems trying to deal with ANYTHING! For example, I’m sitting on a 10 minute wait on a CALL BACK! Still haven’t spoken to a person. They charged me a $40 late fee when I have automatic payments. Took 3-4 months to get it refunded. I updated mine and my fiancés phones and they took over a month to process our returned phones in their warehouse so I was charged over $100 more than my usual bill and it took 4-5 months for me to get that money refunded. I had to call every months my bill was paid and I saw no refund as promised. I’m constantly passed back and forth from agent to agent and nobody knows what’s going on. I have to retell my story to every new agent on the phone and each one says they’re the last person I’ll have to speak to and they will resolve my problem. Not true ever! Now thirteen months it’s on hold for a call back. 
I called two weeks ago to file a claim because our ipad just keeps shutting off for no reason. Was told I have a claim and I should receive an email within 24 hours. Never got anything. This is why I’m calling back now. I’ve been on the phone with Assurant and T-Mobile for the last hour being passed back and forth. Call back is now at 15 minutes. WHY DID YOU CALL MY PHONE IF NOBODY IS THERE TO TALK TO ME. I  hate this company. Do better! Wow, I just got someone on the phone and they hung up once I calmly said I have had problems getting someone on the phone. Screw you whoever you were.

Worst experience I have had in a very long time.  If you are considering switching to TMobil, I would consider this carefully.  After switching to TMobil with 3 lines my monthly charges were promised to be appx. $96.00.  After 4 months of spending hours and hours with TMobile customer reps and being told that they would get the new monthly charge of $140 fixed.  They never did.  Now, we have had an additional letdown in the phones (OnePlus 9) which we purchased 3 of.  Finally my wife's phone was deemed defective and a new one has been ordered.  My personal phone has had many, many ongoing problems that no one can account for.  
So, like I said, if you are thinking of switching to TMobil... good luck!

This company has employees who have zero knowledge in what they are supposed to be doing far as customer service. I sat on the phone literally all day trying to get a galaxy s22 plus activated.  This is what you call poor communication and the experience has been very frustrating.

THE WORST CUSTOMER SERVICE AND STAFF EVER.  
The person at the mission viejo store in the mission viejo mall that I dealt with last night was beyond worthless, not to mention rude.  Absolutely the most unhelpful person I have dealt with in a long time.  
I went to a different store and got my return accomplished, but waited over 30 minutes.  There were 4 people working and a good 6 people walking in and out of the front and back doors doing nothing.  There were at least 6 people in line and no one seemed to care.  
Furthermore, I was asked to try their internet service 30 days FOR FREE.  I have been trying to return their modem for days now with no success due to inept and useless staff.  I was told today that the trial period has a 14 days and I would be charged!!!!  He did say to call customer service and try to get a refund.  I’m standing right there in the store, just returned the item and he can do nothing for me but to tell me to call customer service….really?  What the heck are they there for then?
The only helpful person in all this was a person on chat last night that helped me with what the staff at mission viejo flatly refused to do.  
Negative stars are warranted for this company’s customer service overall.

Customer first? I wanted to sign up with them early July, and called their sales line to get started. They shipped a SIM card that never arrived. UPS requested a signature and I was not home for a few days. That got sent back to sender. Then more than a month later a random charge popped up on my credit card. No bill or whatsoever to try to justify it. I called to check and they told me they couldn’t verify me because my account was never activated and I didn’t have the passcode. They told me to go to a store and get verified. Took like 2 hours and numerous reps to dig into the issue. The store rep were very helpful though, they had to deal with the reps on the phone too. It got resolved eventually but man what a challenge and shady business. I’d recommend to stay away from them.

I'm NOT a customer because after 3 weeks of online attempts, literally 3 hours of telephone hand-offs from one unintelligible broken english speaker, ( I'm in the U.S.) to another,  2.5 hours of online chats, (same lack of language skills/grammar). I've submitted the online application for new service and it thinks I'm a customer already. Never have been. Then it instructs me to either "chat" or call customer care. After an interminable wait listening to crummy music, I get one of the above-referenced "experts" (their term, not mine) who will ask for the same information that I submitted in the online application while up selling, non-stop...offering additional services, lines, insurance, etc. repeatedly, before disclosing a $70.00 connection charge. ($35. per line on the Magenta 55+ plan) ONLY because that's required when you interact with any sales 'expert'. That includes anyone in one of their offices, too, I checked. So, the online application: thinks I'm already a customer and none of the 'experts' can fix it. The best advice the last manager could give me after making sure that the "ghost" account is cancelled is to wait 90 days at which time their system will automatically purge my name, address, etc. and allegedly allow me to proceed!? I wondered if signing up under my wife's name, etc. would work or if she'd already be a customer ,too. She wasn't but after all the required info. was plugged in, Its giving me a "problem with address message" WITH THE USPS APPROVED ADDRESS. What a third world comedy. If this is what trying to establish service is like, what should I expect down the road? Seriously? I thought I'd be smart and save a few bucks every month.. Guess I'm not cheap enough to continue being shell-shocked by the absolute worst customer no-service I've ever experienced. I'll pay a little more to Verizon or somebody else.

I had a horrible experience with nearly all levels of T-mobile retail and customer service support when switching phone providers. My family was switching and my father had made all of us authorized users. When I was trying to switch, T-mobile flagged me for fraud somehow. I had to wait on the fraud line for nearly two hours in the store before they could confirm my identity. I had my dad try to call them but they would only allow identity confirmation through my dad's home phone. He had to stop what he was doing, drive home, and answer the fraud dept's call from the home phone. They finally cleared me. I almost got my new phone and then I was flagged for fraud again. Throughout this whole experience, the retail employee handling my transition was extremely unhappy, unhelpful, and grouchy. He acted as if this horrible situation were happening to him, and not something his company was causing to me. He said he was mad because this whole ordeal forced him to miss his lunch break. When I, as the customer, missed a huge commitment I had promised to attend because of the length of this frivolous fraud delay. Then I go home, and T-mobile did not transfer my number correctly. They had given me a temporary phone number, which was not so temporary. People could call my actual number, but texts were not going through. I could only text from this temporary number, but none of my contacts had it and didn't know who it was. When people would text my actual number, texts wouldn't go through. I spent a cumulative near 10 hours (TEN HOURS) on the phone with over 10 different customer service representatives trying to fix this issue. They even tried to blame Apple, and forced me to connect with Apple's technical team. I spent an hour and half on the phone with apple trying to fix the issue, but Apple determined it was T-mobile's fault. Finally, I get a hold of supposedly "the best" T-mobile technical support person. She had me go in and manually update my actual number in some hidden area of the settings bar, deleting the temporary number T-mobile had given me. This took all of 30 seconds. I see no reason why this couldn't have been done in the first place. The retail person in the store could have done it I presume. Yet, I instead had to waste TEN HOURS over two days dealing with this, explaining my conundrum multiple times to multiple customer services representatives from the beginning each time. It was the worst, most frustrating experience I've ever had dealing with a corporation. When their customer service person called to apologize, he said he could comp me $10. TEN DOLLARS, for ten wasted hours of my time, and a work commitment T-mobile forced me to miss the prior day. I had to laugh out loud at this. It was incredibly offensive to me. I'm deeply disappointed in T-mobile and think they should compensate me for my lost time and their horrendous incompetence.

I bought a new phone and was told I could get a smartwatch for free. I impulsively said yes but then realized, nothing is for free, tax, connection charge, $10 a month additional charge, after a day I returned it and was going to be charged a $20 restock fee, but the WONDERFUL customer service agent MARTIN in Myrtle Beach Seaboard Store helped me to receive a credit. He was incredibly kind and patient and listened to my frustration and helped me out! I will stick with tmobile!
terrible customer service!!!

I don't understand when I saw a 2 rating.  My experience with T-Mobile in Pace, FL was outstanding!  First time to get internet.  Was paying $80 to AT&T and now I pay $50.  The person who help me at that time explained everything.  When I got home, I didn't understand something and I called and the problem was solved.  Was paying Straightalk  $90 and I am still paying $90....BUT...so many more bennies,  I now get Netflix monthly for always, Paramount free for a year and Apple for a year.  Might  only be for a year but I will still be saving $16 on Netflix alone a monthly after that.  My rep was Taylor.  Talk about patience,  She took care of everything from setting up my phone, to setting up a new phone for my husband. Now anyone who has changed carriers knows the time and frustration of doing it.  She took care of all of it and I wish I could contact
 T-Mobile to leave a review there.  She is an outstanding rep for T-Mobile.  That stores environment is relaxful, friendly and everybody seems so patient.  I give Taylor a 2 thumbs up for a job well done,

Ive had tmobile off and on for several years. Every term ive had with them prior to this last year. Had been above and beyond. No issues, maybe service outside the state i was in. Other than that great. This year, i got the samsung flip z. I went into the store to return and get a different phone. Expressed what i did not like. I put 400 some dollars down. I did not have receipt so nothing could be done. Fast forward, more complications. I call tmobile. They tell me i have to pay off the phone and put money down for a new phone. I asked if there is anything they cam do for me. I invested a lot of time and money. The mobile app i believe they shadow banned me from. It has not worked in a week now. Everytime i sign in and use the website. The besite goes down. I tried adding a line for a security camera. They could not let me do it. Then the site went down. Very unfortunate. I had a similar incident with Apple products. The customer service, is just not there, anywhere. These days.
